title: SandwichAttack (vigil-v1)
description: Solana sandwich-detector output schema, version vigil-v1. Each detection serializes one row of this shape; lines prefixed with `_header` or `_heartbeat` are framing and not validated against this schema.

slot.description: Slot number where the sandwich occurred

estimated_attacker_profit.description: Naive profit estimate (quote token, smallest unit): `backrun.amount_out - frontrun.amount_in`. Wrong for multi-token pairs and doesn't account for price-impact dynamics. Kept for backwards compatibility with rule-based consumers; prefer `attacker_profit`.

victim_loss_lamports.description: AMM-correct victim loss in the quote-token smallest unit: the difference between what the victim would have received without the frontrun and what they actually got. Populated by `pool-state` enrichment when a [`PoolStateLookup`](../../pool-state) is available; `None` if pool state couldn't be resolved. Mirrors Vigil `mev_attack.victim_loss_lamports`.

victim_loss_lamports_lower.description: Lower bound of the victim-loss confidence interval (Tier 3.3), in the same quote-token smallest unit as `victim_loss_lamports`. Width is derived from the worst per-step model/parser disagreement (`Signal::InvariantResidual`); both bounds are `None` when observations are missing on every step or the residual is pathological (≥100%). Pairs with `victim_loss_lamports_upper` — emit either both or neither.

victim_loss_lamports_upper.description: Upper bound of the victim-loss confidence interval. See `victim_loss_lamports_lower` for the derivation and pairing rules.

attacker_profit.description: AMM-correct attacker gross profit via counterfactual replay. Populated alongside `victim_loss_lamports`. Can differ significantly from `estimated_attacker_profit` — the re-classification from "naive profitable" to "AMM unprofitable" is the signal that distinguishes real extraction from false positives. Mirrors Vigil `mev_attack.attacker_profit`.

price_impact_bps.description: Price impact of the frontrun in basis points (|Δprice| / price_before × 10_000).

frontrun_slot.description: For cross-slot sandwiches: slot of the frontrun tx.

backrun_slot.description: For cross-slot sandwiches: slot of the backrun tx.

confidence.description: Composite confidence score [0.0, 1.0] incorporating provenance + economics + victim plausibility.

net_profit.description: Net economic profit for the attacker after costs (fees + tip + gas). `None` if cost data unavailable.

evidence.description: Structured reasoning trace. Each emitted detection carries the set of orthogonal signals that fired, so a reader can independently verify why we flagged the triplet. `None` on attacks emitted before evidence wiring (maintains JSONL backwards compatibility).

amm_replay.description: Snapshot of the AMM replay used to compute `victim_loss_lamports` / `attacker_profit`. Exposed so readers can recompute victim loss themselves from the raw pool arithmetic. `None` when pool-state enrichment didn't run or the DEX isn't supported.

whirlpool_replay.description: Whirlpool-specific replay trace — populated only when `dex` is `OrcaWhirlpool` and pool-state enrichment ran successfully. Mirrors [`AmmReplayTrace`] in spirit: lets a reader recompute victim loss / attacker profit from the raw concentrated-liquidity arithmetic. Distinct field rather than an enum so the existing `amm_replay` shape stays backward-compatible for ConstantProduct consumers.

dlmm_replay.description: Meteora DLMM-specific replay trace — populated only when `dex` is `MeteoraDlmm` and Phase 2 cross-bin replay ran successfully. Tracks `active_id` transitions instead of Whirlpool's sqrt_price/liquidity/tick triple, since DLMM's discrete-bin model captures a leg's effect entirely in which bin became active. Distinct field rather than a `whirlpool_replay` enum variant for the same backward-compat reason.

attack_signature.description: Canonical attack signature mirrored to Vigil's `mev_attack.signature`. Today set to the victim tx signature so per-victim receipts can FK by `attack_signature`. `None` on records emitted before this field existed.

timestamp_ms.description: Block timestamp in milliseconds since epoch (Vigil `mev_attack.timestamp_ms`). Populated when the upstream block source reports `block_time`; `None` for synthetic test fixtures.

attack_type.description: Stable attack-type taxonomy (Vigil `mev_attack.type`). `None` on records predating this enum.

severity.description: Vigil-style severity bucket. Derivation requires pool-reserve context the caller has at enrichment time, so this is set externally rather than by `finalize_for_vigil`.

slot_leader.description: Slot leader at the victim's slot (Vigil `validator_identity`). `None` until Tier 2 enrichment lands.

is_wide_sandwich.description: `true` when the frontrun and backrun are separated by intervening txs (`tx_index` gap > 1) or by more than one slot. Mirrors Vigil's `sandwich_detail.is_wide_sandwich`. Defaults to `false` on legacy records.

receipts.description: Per-victim receipt projection for Vigil's `mev_receipt` table. Today a single attack maps to one receipt; `Vec` shape allows wide-sandwich variants with multiple victims to fan out without changing the schema. Empty on legacy records.

victim_amount_out.description: Mirrors Vigil `mev_attack.victim_amount_out`. Promoted from `victim.amount_out` (what the victim actually received).

victim_amount_out_expected.description: Mirrors Vigil `mev_attack.victim_amount_out_expected`. Promoted from `amm_replay.counterfactual_victim_out` — what the victim would have received without the frontrun. `None` when AMM replay didn't run.

SwapEvent.description: A single swap event extracted from a transaction

amount_in.description: Amount of token going in (in smallest unit)

tx_index.description: Transaction index within the block (determines ordering)

slot.description: Slot number (needed for cross-slot correlation). `None` for same-block only.

fee.description: Transaction fee in lamports. `None` if not available from parser context.

oneOf.description: Buying the token (SOL/quote -> token)
Selling the token (token -> SOL/quote)

BundleProvenance.description:
Jito bundle relationship of a sandwich triplet.

Determines confidence: `AtomicBundle` ≈ 100%, `Organic` needs economic proof.

oneOf.description: All 3 txs in the same Jito bundle — near-certain sandwich.
Frontrun + backrun in the same bundle, victim separate — high confidence.
All 3 are in bundles, but different ones — medium confidence.
None of the txs are in a bundle — needs economic/victim filters.

DetectionEvidence.description: Structured trace of everything the detector considered for one detection.

failing.description: Signals whose verdict was Fail. Empty by default — populated only when `--evidence-mode full` is set on the CLI.

informational.description: Signals classified as Informational (context, not a vote).

ensemble_agreement.description: Fraction of ensemble categories with at least one passing signal, in `[0.0, 1.0]`. Denominator is [`ENSEMBLE_CATEGORY_COUNT`].

categories_fired.description: Integer companion to `ensemble_agreement` — `categories_with_pass` count. Easier to quote in prose ("4 of 5 signals fired").

Signal.description:
One of the orthogonal signals considered when scoring a candidate sandwich.

Each variant carries the raw evidence value so a reader can judge the call without re-running the detector. Signals are grouped into [`SignalCategory`] for ensemble-agreement counting — a detection that "fires" across more independent categories is more credible than one that scores highly in a single category.

oneOf.description: Victim tx is tightly sandwiched between frontrun and backrun in tx order.
All three txs landed in the same slot.
Cross-slot sandwich; `slot_distance = backrun_slot - frontrun_slot`.
Jito bundle membership of the triplet, plus the raw bundle id if known.
Naive (same-token) profit decomposition: gross revenue, estimated cost, net.
AMM-correct attacker profit from pool-state replay.
Counterfactual victim loss and frontrun price impact from AMM replay.

`1 - (actual_victim_out / counterfactual_victim_out)`, clamped to [0, 1]. A value near 1.0 means the victim received almost nothing relative to the counterfactual — strong AMM-replay evidence of extraction.
Ratio of `victim.amount_in / frontrun.amount_in`. Higher is more plausible (victim is economically meaningful relative to the attacker's position).
Whether the victim signer appears in the known-attacker list (attacker-on- attacker detections are almost always false positives).

Identity-linkage evidence: an SPL Token `SetAuthority` instruction transferred control of an account from `from` to `to` within the victim's window. Used by the Authority-Hop heuristic to fuse a frontrun signed by `from` with a backrun signed by `to` even though the two wallets look unrelated. `authority_tx` is the signature of the tx that carried the SetAuthority — the reader can replay it to audit the link.

Per-step model fidelity check (Tier 3.2). Compares what our AMM `apply_swap` would predict for `step`'s output, given the reserves we used at that point in the replay, against `SwapEvent.amount_out` extracted from chain logs. `residual_bps = (predicted - observed) / observed × 10_000`, signed.

Near-zero residual means our reserve reconstruction and constant- product math agree with the chain's outcome — downstream replay numbers (`AmmProfit`, `VictimLoss`, `ReplayConfidence`) are trust- worthy. Large |residual| means we missed something (wrong pre-state, non-AMM CPI inside the tx, or a DEX whose math we don't model correctly) and the replay-derived figures should be discounted. Routed to the Economic category so a Fail here weighs against the other replay signals.

Counterfactual attacker outcome (Tier 3.5): what the attacker would have netted if the victim's tx had not happened. Computed by replaying `frontrun → backrun` directly on the post-frontrun pool, skipping the victim. Both values are in quote-token smallest units, normalised the same way as `attacker_profit`.

A pure sandwich *needs* the victim to extract value, so the canonical shape is `with_victim > 0` and `without_victim ≤ 0`. When `without_victim` is also strongly positive, the candidate is more likely an arbitrage that happened to bracket an unrelated swap — the signal Fails so the ensemble downweights it.

End-state model-vs-chain check (Tier 3.1). Compares `amm_replay.reserves_post_back` (what our replay says the pool's vaults should hold after the backrun) against the chain's actual post-backrun vault balances, taken from the backrun tx's `post_token_balances`. `divergence_bps` is the larger of the two side-wise relative deltas (base, quote), expressed in basis points.

This is the headline self-proof for the replay engine: where `InvariantResidual` checks each step against parser-observed `amount_out`, this checks the *final* reconstructed pool state against on-chain ground truth. A small divergence is positive confirmation that every reserves transition we computed lined up with the chain — which means the upstream `victim_loss` / `attacker_profit_real` numbers can be trusted at face value. A large divergence indicates we missed an instruction (rebalance, fee withdrawal, multi-hop CPI) and the replay-derived figures should be discounted. Routed to the Economic category. Pass on its own isn't evidence *for* a sandwich — that comes from the AMM-replay signals — so this signal only votes Fail (or Informational) per the `InvariantResidual` precedent.

ReplayStep.description: Replay step where an [`Signal::InvariantResidual`] was measured.

AmmReplayTrace.description:
Snapshot of the AMM replay used to compute victim loss / attacker profit.

Exposed on `SandwichAttack` so a reader can recompute victim loss from the raw pool arithmetic using only these fields (reserves at each step + fee).

reserves_pre.description: (base, quote) reserves immediately before the frontrun.

reserves_post_front.description: Reserves after the frontrun, before the victim.

reserves_post_victim.description: Reserves after the victim, before the backrun.

reserves_post_back.description: Reserves after the backrun (end of the triplet).

spot_price_post_front.description: Spot price after the frontrun — used for `price_impact_bps`.

counterfactual_victim_out.description: What the victim would have received if the frontrun hadn't happened.

fee_num.description: Numerator of the pool's constant-product fee (e.g. 25 for 25bps). Matches `PoolConfig::fee_num` source type so no narrowing cast happens en route to the trace.

WhirlpoolReplayTrace.description:
Whirlpool (concentrated-liquidity) replay trace — the analogue of [`AmmReplayTrace`] for the V3-style swap math added in Tier 3.4.

Surfaces the per-step sqrt_price / liquidity / tick the replay walked through, so a reader can reconstruct the swap arithmetic the same way `AmmReplayTrace` lets them re-derive constant-product victim_loss from raw reserves.

`u128` fields (sqrt_price, liquidity) serialise as **base-10 decimal strings** to preserve precision in JSON consumers — JS's `number` is a 53-bit float and would lose information on values past `2^53`. Q64.64 sqrt_prices sit around `2^64`, well past that. TS-side parsers should use `BigInt(...)` to read these.

sqrt_price_pre.description: Q64.64 sqrt(price) immediately before the frontrun.

sqrt_price_post_front.description: Sqrt-price after the frontrun, before the victim.

sqrt_price_post_victim.description: Sqrt-price after the victim, before the backrun.

sqrt_price_post_back.description: Sqrt-price after the backrun (end of the triplet).

liquidity_pre.description: Active liquidity at each step. Constant within one tick band, but changes when a leg's swap walks past an initialised tick boundary (LP positions activate / deactivate per V3 conventions).

tick_current_pre.description: Tick (price bucket) at each step. `floor(log_{1.0001}(price))`. Fits in `i32` per Whirlpool's own layout; safe in JSON.

counterfactual_victim_out.description: What the victim would have received without the frontrun.

fee_num.description: LP fee fraction. Whirlpool uses `fee_num` in hundredths-of-bps against `fee_den = 1_000_000` (e.g. `3000 / 1_000_000` = 30 bps). `u64` matches `PoolConfig::fee_num` and the DLMM trace, removing a narrowing `as u32` cast at the construction site.

MeteoraDlmmReplayTrace.description:
Per-step DLMM (Meteora Liquidity Book) replay trace surfaced on `SandwichAttack.dlmm_replay`. Mirrors [`WhirlpoolReplayTrace`] in shape but tracks `active_id` transitions instead of sqrt_price / liquidity / tick — DLMM's discrete-bin model means a leg's effect is fully captured by which bin became active.

`bin_price_pre` is Q64.64; serialised as a base-10 decimal string per the same JS-BigInt rationale that drove [`WhirlpoolReplayTrace`]. `active_id` fits in i32 (per the on-chain `MIN_BIN_ID` / `MAX_BIN_ID = ±443_636` cap), safe in JSON.

active_id_pre.description: Active bin id immediately before the frontrun executed.

active_id_post_front.description: Active bin id after the frontrun, before the victim.

active_id_post_victim.description: Active bin id after the victim, before the backrun.

active_id_post_back.description: Active bin id after the backrun (end of the triplet).

bin_price_pre.description: Q64.64 spot price at the pre-frontrun moment (the active bin's price). DLMM bin prices are static per bin (set at pool creation via the `bin_step` formula), so this is the canonical pre-frontrun spot.

counterfactual_victim_out.description: What the victim would have received without the frontrun.

bin_step.description: Bin step in basis points (`25 = 0.25%`). Static per pool; surfaced here so a reader can derive any bin's price from `bin_id` without re-deriving the pool config.

fee_num.description: LP fee fraction. DLMM uses `fee_num / 1_000_000_000` units. Phase 1/2: base fee only (`base_factor * bin_step * 10 * 10^bf_power`). Phase 3: this carries the *static base* portion of the rate; the variable component is surfaced separately via `variable_fee_rate_*` so a reader can decompose the total. Capped at 10% post-sum.

volatility_accumulator_pre.description: Volatility accumulator carried by the pool snapshot (`v_parameters.volatility_accumulator`). On-chain `update_references` does *not* mutate this field — it only resets `volatility_reference` based on elapsed time — so this surfaces the raw account-snapshot value. The first bin of the cross-bin walk reads it indirectly through `update_volatility_accumulator`'s `delta_id = |index_reference - active_id|` formula; subsequent bins overwrite it.

volatility_accumulator_post_front.description: Volatility accumulator after the frontrun walk. Equals `pre + bins_crossed * 10_000` (capped at `max_volatility_accumulator`). The biggest accumulator value the victim's per-bin fee will ever see in the standard frontrun→victim→backrun sequence.

variable_fee_rate_pre.description: Variable-fee numerator at the pre-frontrun accumulator value (over `fee_den`). `0` when the pool's `variable_fee_control` is 0 or the accumulator is 0. Add to `fee_num` to get the pre-frontrun total fee rate.

variable_fee_rate_post_front.description: Variable-fee numerator at the post-frontrun accumulator. Lets a reader see how much the frontrun's bin walk inflated the fee the victim paid.

token_x_transfer_fee_bps.description: Token-2022 transfer fee on token X, in basis points over `10_000`. `None` for legacy SPL Token mints (the common case for Phase 1/2 corpus pools). Surfaced symmetrically for X/Y rather than in/out so the trace is direction-agnostic.

token_y_transfer_fee_bps.description: Token-2022 transfer fee on token Y, in basis points.

AttackType.description: Stable attack-type taxonomy mirrored to Vigil's `mev_attack.type` column. Today the detector emits `Sandwich` (contiguous, single-slot) or `WideSandwich` (intervening txs or cross-slot). The remaining variants are placeholders for future tiers — backrun-only exploitation and the authority-hop heuristic — so the schema doesn't churn when those land.

ConfidenceLevel.description: Text bucket of the float `confidence` score (Vigil `mev_receipt.loss_confidence`).

MevReceipt.description:
Per-victim projection of a [`SandwichAttack`] mirroring Vigil's `mev_receipt` table columns. BE-managed columns (receipt id, USD pricing, protection metadata, share urls, created_at) are intentionally omitted — those are filled by the Vigil backend, not the detector.

A single sandwich today produces exactly one receipt; the field on `SandwichAttack` is `Vec<MevReceipt>` so wide-sandwich variants with multiple victims can fan out without breaking the schema later.

attack_signature.description: FK to `mev_attack.signature` — set to whatever the parent attack uses as its canonical id (today: the victim tx signature).

token_in_mint.description: Input token mint. `None` when the parser didn't resolve the quote-side mint of the pair (today only the non-quote token is tracked).

token_out_mint.description: Output token mint. Same caveat as `token_in_mint`.

expected_amount_out.description: Counterfactual victim output (what they would have received without the frontrun). `None` when AMM-replay enrichment didn't run.

slippage.description: `(expected − actual) / expected`, in `[0.0, 1.0]`. `None` when expected is unknown.

mev_detected.description: Always `true` on emitted receipts; column kept so the BE can persist negative receipts later without a schema change.

loss_amount.description: Loss in quote-token smallest units. `None` when AMM replay didn't run.

loss_amount_lower.description: Lower / upper bounds of the victim-loss confidence interval (Tier 3.3), mirrored from `SandwichAttack.victim_loss_lamports_{lower,upper}`. Same quote-token unit as `loss_amount`. Both `None` when no observation-based CI was derivable.

validator_identity.description: Vigil `validator_identity` — slot leader at the victim's slot. `None` until Tier 2 enrichment is wired up.
